title: Switching to Renewable Energy
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The world is increasingly turning towards (0) renewable sources of energy as a cleaner alternative to fossil fuels. Coal and oil release (17) poisonous gases that contribute to global warming and damage air quality. Reducing our (18) dependence on these fuels is considered essential by most climate scientists. Solar panels and wind turbines have improved in (19) efficiency over recent decades, making them far more practical. The (20) advancement of battery technology has also helped to solve the problem of storing energy when the sun is not shining. Governments are making considerable (21) investment in wind farms and solar parks to meet growing energy demands. The (22) reliability of renewable sources has improved greatly in recent years. There is now (23) global agreement that tackling climate change requires switching away from fossil fuels. Although renewable technology was once (24) expensive, prices have fallen dramatically, making it accessible to many more countries.

example.text: The world is increasingly turning towards (0) RENEWABLE sources of energy as a cleaner alternative to fossil fuels. (RENEW)

questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'gases'; 'poison' becomes 'poisonous' by adding the suffix '-ous'.
A noun is needed after 'our'; 'depend' becomes 'dependence' by adding the suffix '-ence'.
A noun is needed after 'in'; 'efficient' becomes 'efficiency' by changing '-ent' to '-ency'.
A noun is needed after 'The'; 'advance' becomes 'advancement' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed after 'considerable'; 'invest' becomes 'investment' by adding the suffix '-ment'.
A noun is needed after 'The'; 'rely' → 'reliable' → 'reliability' by changing '-able' to '-ability'.
An adjective is needed before 'agreement'; 'globe' becomes 'global' by adding the suffix '-al'.
An adjective is needed after 'once'; 'expense' becomes 'expensive' by adding the suffix '-ive'.
